Anubhuti
Khare.

I tend to think a lot about how people interpret a product while they're using it. When something doesn't make sense, I can't leave it as-is — I dig into it, simplify it, and make sure it's actually usable. My work involves turning complex systems into documentation that feels clear, structured, and reliable.

A little about me

Clarity is a craft.

I'm a Technical Writer with a B.Tech in Computer Science — which means I'm equally comfortable reading a system design doc and asking an engineer why their error message makes no sense to a human.

My work sits at the intersection of technical depth and human clarity. I've spent 3+ years building documentation systems, owning content lifecycles end-to-end, and making complex SaaS products feel approachable to the people who use them every day.

Currently at Hevodata, where I work on API docs, product documentation, and UX writing for a data pipeline platform used by thousands of teams globally. Before that, at Whatfix, where I owned docs for a suite of enterprise SaaS products and mentored other writers.

Off the clock, I'm raising two very chaotic kittens and writing things that have nothing to do with software.

Great documentation should feel like a conversation, not a manual.
I enjoy turning "this makes no sense" into "oh wait, that's actually clean."
Actively exploring AI-first documentation workflows and faster content systems.
I care about clarity more than cleverness — but I enjoy both.
I create video documentation — walkthroughs, tutorials, and interactive product demos using Camtasia, ActivePresenter, and Adobe Captivate.
I romanticize sunsets, rain, and well-written documentation equally.

Where I've been

Professional Experience

Nov 2024
— Present
Technical Writer
Hevodata ↗
No-code data pipeline SaaS platform used by 2,000+ data teams globally.
  • Develop and maintain product documentation, UX microcopy, and API/developer docs for a fast-moving, highly technical data integration platform.
  • Collaborate daily with engineering and product teams to validate features, reproduce edge cases, and ensure documentation accuracy prior to each release.
  • Led the migration of 89+ Entity Relationship Diagrams (ERDs) to dbdocs.io using DBML workflows, standardizing schema documentation across the platform.
  • Spearheaded glossary management, interactive video creation, full content audits, and CS ticket analysis — all within first 90 days.
  • Created interactive product walkthroughs and tutorial videos using ActivePresenter and Adobe Captivate.
  • Maintain all documentation via Git-based docs-as-code workflows with cross-functional review cycles.
Jun 2022
— Oct 2024
ID2 — Information Developer
Whatfix ↗
Leading Digital Adoption Platform (DAP) serving enterprise SaaS customers globally.
  • Owned end-to-end documentation lifecycle for product features — from early design review to post-release support content.
  • Authored technical specs, API docs, and user-facing support content using Document360 and Confluence.
  • Created infographics, walkthrough videos, flowcharts, and UI illustrations using Camtasia, Figma, Canva, and Adobe tools.
  • Trained and mentored both junior and senior technical writers, contributing to team quality standards.
  • Participated in product design and usability reviews, contributing structured UX writing feedback.

What I built

Documentation Tools

Six tools built to solve real problems I ran into as a technical writer. Each is a deployable web app.

⚡
API Docs Explorer
An interactive mock API browser. Explore endpoints, fire test requests, and see live syntax-highlighted responses — no backend needed.
Open tool →
✍️
Style Guide Linter
Paste your documentation draft and get instant feedback: passive voice, jargon, weak phrases, sentence length — with a clarity score.
Open tool →
📋
Changelog Generator
Paste raw dev notes or Jira titles. Toggle between internal (full detail) and external (user-friendly) versions. Exports Markdown.
Open tool →
☑️
Doc Review Checklist
A weighted 45-item audit checklist across accuracy, structure, readability, style, accessibility, and UX writing. Exports JSON for AI auditing.
Open tool →
🎬
Video Storyboard Generator
If this doc were a video, here's how it would actually make sense. Breaks your content into scenes, flags where users get lost, and suggests zoom-ins and animations.
Open tool →
😤
User Simulator
Annoying Mode On · AI-Powered
If a confused, slightly irritated user read your doc, this is what they'd ask. Simulates 4 personas, tags confusion points, and gives a clarity score.
Open tool →
My creative writing

Words I've Made

A Wanderer's Ballad
English · Song · Love & travel
Read ↓
In the quiet moments, when the world hushed to a whisper, I couldn't help but reflect on the words spoken to me long ago. Destiny, they said, was my eternal companion, my guiding star. It was a promise of new lands, exotic tastes, and an insatiable love for adventure that coursed through my veins. There's something intoxicating about the anonymity of a foreign city, like the empty pages of a journal yearning to be filled with ink. Under a foreign sky, I could rewrite my beginnings and craft new endings, each step a revelation of self. No plans, no fixed destinations, no expectations; they warned me that I'd never be content with less. But what they didn't tell me was whether I'd be fortunate enough to embark on this adventure with another soul, a kindred spirit seeking the same uncharted territories of the heart. We'd start in the heart of India, the vibrant and chaotic streets of Delhi. Hand in hand, we'd navigate the bustling bazaars, savoring the scent of spices and the kaleidoscope of colors that surrounded us. We'd visit the Red Fort, feeling the weight of centuries of history in its ancient walls. "Come with me to Greece," I'd whisper, feeling the cool breeze off Santorini's white stairs. I'd extend my hand, and together, we'd take the plunge into the crisp turquoise sea of Mykonos, letting the current carry us where it willed. We'd let our journey take us to the shores of Sorrento in Italy, savoring fresh gelato, and feeding each other bites of sweet indulgence. Amidst laughter, we'd roam the Italian countryside, intoxicated by the heady aroma of vineyards. In Paris, we'd seal our love on the Bridge of Love, locking our destiny with a key tossed into the flowing river, a silent prayer whispered on parted lips. And with closed eyes, we'd spin around, our hearts alight with anticipation. Learning to navigate the wrong side of the road, we'd venture to Poland for a festival of lights, releasing lanterns that carried our love higher and higher, until they became stars in the night sky. A train would carry us to Madrid, where we'd dance the night away in a local cantina, lost in the vibrant colors and passion of Spain. When raindrops fell, we'd find shelter in the streets of Agueda, Portugal, and then, in a moment of impulsive delight, we'd embrace the downpour, spinning and kissing until our hearts raced. Together, we'd trace our footsteps through Africa, savoring the citrus sun atop Cape Town's skyscrapers, standing in awe of the breathtaking Victoria Falls. We'd walk barefoot and bleeding on the earth of Mount Kilimanjaro, each step a testament to our determination. And I'd learn to surf in Cape Town, your love guiding me through the waves, a metaphor for our shared adventures. We'd traverse the streets of Tokyo, the neon lights casting an otherworldly glow upon our eager faces. Lost in translation, yet found in the vibrant culture, we'd savor sushi beneath a cherry blossom canopy and soak in the serenity of Kyoto's bamboo forests. In Vietnam, we'd cruise along the emerald waters of Halong Bay, where limestone islets rose like sentinels from the sea. The tranquility of this natural wonder would be our respite from the world's frenetic pace. As we flew back home, you'd rest your head on my shoulder, capturing our adventures with Polaroids, paper, and ink. But you'd remind me that our journey didn't end there; home was not defined by four walls, but by the laughter within them, the moments of solace in a warm breeze, and the memories beneath twinkling lights on our porch. Home, you'd tell me, wasn't a fixed location but a feeling nestled deep within us, within each other. It was the cradle of your hand, weathered and creased, holding mine through the years. With every sunrise, I'd find new desires, and I'd promise you a life that never grew stale. My heart would spill forth in sugar-coated rhymes and passionate affirmations. You, my love, were deserving of every fervent word etched upon my soul, the greatest treasure I'd ever discovered. You'd reassure me of my beauty, marking my paperback heart with your indelible presence. Our story, etched in the creases of our smile lines and the folds of our wrinkles, would be passed down through generations. And if ever I ran out of pages to fill, you'd hold every word I uttered safe in your heart. You, my love, would be the walking journal I'd always needed, reciting how my words danced in your soul, reflected my passion, and understood my pain. A living testament that I had lived and loved, for you were, and forever would be, my grandest adventure.
Almost Forgot
English · Nostalgic
Read ↓
Somedays you will love being an adult to drive your car on open streets to spend your money on late night treats to live your life as you please but every so often you wish it were all a dream and you wish you wake up soon and cry to not go to school I remember the kids we used to be when summer days were spent in parks blissfully unaware of the melting earth when getting up was easy after a fall the ground wasn't even that far I remember the kids we used to be who knew love from SRK movies Somedays you will love being an adult but every so often you'll think back to old days you'll miss the naivety and yearn to return to the days when you yearned for maturity
फालतू के लम्हे
Hindi · Midnight poetry
Read ↓
सुभा का क्या है? वो रोज ही बिन बुलाए दस्तक दे ही देती है। जैसे कोई ज़िद्दी मेहमान जो मुँह उठाकर चला आता है। दोपहर... ना गर्म है, ना ठंडी। बस एक लिज़लिज़ी सी परछाईं जो टपकती रहती है। शाम भी यूँही सूरज को जाता देख भागी चली आती है। हर रोज़ एक ही रंग में रंगी। बस ये आधी रात के कुछ लम्हे ही तो हैं, चुराए हुए थे। इन लम्हों में ज़िन्दगी का अलग ही मज़ा है। क्यूँकि ये मिलते नहीं... ये छीने जाते हैं। मगर बेगानी नींद जैसे सुभा की दलाल बनके कमबख़्त आ ही जाती है।
Crowd Control
English · Love & longing
Read ↓
Walls blur, voices drown in their own echoes, and the world becomes a painting smudged by time. All I ever truly search for is not the room, but the tremble my heart gives when it believes you might be in it. There is a stillness that wraps around my ribs like ivy curling in grief… the kind that only blooms in the absence of your face. What is a crowd if not a labyrinth of strangers between me and the one soul I would cross oceans of silence to find? And what is love if not this constant ache, this quiet, uninvited prayer that your face be the next one I see when the door creaks open? I look for you the way the moon looks for the sea… not loudly, not proudly, but with a pull so ancient, it forgets how to want anything else. Even in joy, my happiness limps because it is missing the weight of your gaze. Even in light, my shadow wanders, asking the sun if it has seen your outline.
Inked
English · Identity · About writing
Read ↓
Literature is all I have ever been. Words, on words, on words– I am left breathless and shaken as poem after poem strikes its way through my heart. I am a shrewd, smiling amalgamation of every character I have ever written, read, or played– I am all. A stage. A writer. An actor. A reader. A viewer. What am I? I am the audience and the performer. Before literature, I was nothing. A blank book, ready to be inked over time and time again. I have filled my pages with words, with ink stains, with everything good– yet painful, Ugly– yet warm. Words define me, they created me. In a twisted way, I am my own tragedy. I am the rise, the peak, the fall– I am both the tragic hero and the tragic villain. Words, on words, on words– What am I? I am the urge to be set free, to be comforted: to know that someone has felt this before. I know nothing. I know everything. These novels, stories, and poems have left me desolate– I am wretched. I am destruction in its finest form. What am I, if not a song for the bards to sing? if not an actor's muse? if not an artist? Words have left me hurt, and yet I turn to them for healing. There is a sort of madness in being an artist. You must be mad to create, to write. You must be mad, utterly out of your mind, to be a poet. Many compare a poet's pen to a soldier's sword– I do not quite agree. The poet's weapon is his madness. It is his madness that forces him to write– to pour his own blood and spill the depths of his own soul onto pages of his own flesh. A poet's work is a wretched little thing. Lines, sonnets, and stanzas written in ruby red. It is the poet's way of cracking open his own ribs and telling the audience: Look at me. Look at my madness, my vulnerability bared raw– and view it as your own. I am mad, yet I am entirely sane. My mind lacks the sort of clearness many view as sanity– but I know I am sane. But I am mad. How can that be? How can that be? A poet's existence is nothing more than irony.
The real bosses

Rosa & Lily

My two most important roommates. I feed them, they judge me. It works.

Rosa
Certified mama's girl
Clingy Cuddly Emotionally intense Needs to be touching me at all times

Rosa is… attached. Like, physically and emotionally. If I'm sitting, she's on me. If I'm walking, she's following. If I close a door, it's betrayal. She just wants to be held, noticed, included in everything. Extremely soft, extremely loving, and slightly dramatic about it.

Lily
Chaos incarnate (should've been a pirate)
Curious Fearless Troublemaker Professional explorer

Lily was not made for a calm, domestic life. She wants adventure, risk, and ideally something she's not supposed to touch. She's the one climbing things, jumping off things, and generally testing the limits of physics and my patience. There's always a moment of silence before I realize she's doing something illegal.

🐾

Together, they're… a lot.

One is glued to me like I'm her entire world. The other is off trying to conquer it. Between the two of them, nothing stays where it was, I'm never alone, and somehow I'm always being supervised.
